title: Agent Guidelines & Rules
content:
# agent guidelines & rules

## mandatory workflow discovery
before starting any planning or coding task, you must search for existing guidelines and reusable workflows:
1. run the discovery cli tool with relevant keywords matching your task:
   ```bash
   node scripts/discover-workflows.js <keyword>
   ```
2. review the matched workflow files and read them to reuse existing scripts, commands, and patterns.
3. if no matching workflow is found, consult the standard phase-specific routers:

- **planning**: [agent-plan.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/plan/agent-plan.md)
- **implementation**: [agent-impl.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/impl/agent-impl.md)
- **verification**: [agent-verify.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/verify/agent-verify.md)
- **debugging**: [agent-debug.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/debug/agent-debug.md)

title: Debugging Phase
content:
# debugging phase

refer to diagnostic workflows:
- logging: [logging-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/debug/logging-wf.md)
- profiling: [profiling-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/debug/profiling-wf.md)
- diagnostics: [diagnostics-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/debug/diagnostics-wf.md)

title: Diagnostics Workflow (`diagnostics-wf`)
content:
# diagnostics workflow (`diagnostics-wf`)

## execution
run system status checks or check database connection parameters to diagnose issues.

title: Logging Workflow (`logging-wf`)
content:
# logging workflow (`logging-wf`)

## execution
use target-specific debug environment variables or print debug logs where needed.
check console outputs in both dev server environments:
- web app: `npm run dev` output.

title: Profiling Workflow (`profiling-wf`)
content:
# profiling workflow (`profiling-wf`)

## execution
profile script runtimes and api endpoints using built-in node.js profilers or console timers.

title: Implementation Phase
content:
# implementation phase

when implementing code changes, refer to the following semantic files:

1. **coding standards & best practices**:
   - refer to: [coding.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/impl/coding.md)

2. **parallel task execution**:
   - refer to: [commands-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/workflows/commands-wf.md)

3. **automation & scripting**:
   - refer to: [automation.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/impl/automation.md)

title: Automation Guidelines
content:
# automation guidelines

- automate repetitive development tasks using npm scripts or custom scripts inside the `scripts/` directory.
- document any new automation workflow under `.agents/*-wf.md`.

title: Coding Guidelines
content:
# coding guidelines

- maintain documentation integrity.
- preserve existing comments and docstrings.
- ensure type correctness and lint verification.
- group updates by component layer (monorepo packages vs apps).

title: Planning Phase
content:
# planning phase

when planning, refer to the following specific guidelines and workflows:

1. **understand repository layout**:
   - run the layout mapping workflow: [tree-package-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/workflows/tree-package-wf.md)

2. **produce implementation plan**:
   - follow formatting rules: [plan-format.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/plan/plan-format.md)

3. **split work into independently verifiable microtasks**:
   - follow precision and microtask rules: [microtasks.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/plan/microtasks.md)

4. **workflow discovery**:
   - reusing and discovering workflows: [discovery.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/plan/discovery.md)
   - cli search tool: [discovery-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/workflows/discovery-wf.md)

title: Workflow Discovery & Decomposition
content:
# workflow discovery & decomposition

## identifying reusable workflows
during the planning phase:
1. determine if a reusable workflow already exists in `.agents/*-wf.md`.
2. if not, evaluate if the sequence of commands can be automated via:
   - an npm script
   - a makefile target or taskfile/justfile task
   - a standalone shell script

## post-execution creation
at the end of any task execution/verification:
1. review the commands and processes performed.
2. if it makes sense to do so (e.g. if the steps are repeatable or could benefit future tasks), create a new `*-wf.md` file in `.agents/`.
3. decompose workflows into smaller, modular sub-workflows if they contain independent, reusable parts.
4. reference these workflows in future plans to avoid repeating instructions and save tokens.

title: Microtask Guidelines
content:
# microtask guidelines

when drafting the implementation plan:
- be extremely deliberate.
- pinpoint with high accuracy what should change in the codebase, detailing exact lines, symbols, or functions.
- avoid high-level or generic summaries.
- list changes as specified microtasks.

title: Implementation Plan Formatting Guidelines
content:
# implementation plan formatting guidelines

## target path
- save the plan to: `<appdatadir>/brain/<conversation-id>/implementation_plan.md`

## structure
the document must follow the standard planning template:
1. **goal description**: brief description of what the change accomplishes.
2. **user review required**: highlight breaking changes, critical design decisions, and warnings.
3. **open questions**: clarifying or design questions.
4. **proposed changes**: grouped by component/package, specifying exact files to modify, create, or delete with links.
5. **verification plan**: automated tests and manual steps.

title: Verification Phase
content:
# verification phase

when verifying your changes:

1. **run automated tests**:
   - refer to: [verify-wf.md](file:///users/shivamjain/documents/antigravity/tribelinks/.agents/verify/verify-wf.md)

title: Verification Workflow (`verify-wf`)
content:
# verification workflow (`verify-wf`)

## execution
run the tests at the very end of any task implementation/verification using:

```bash
npm run test
```

## expected output
- all workspace tests should complete successfully with code 0.

title: Reusable Commands & Execution Guidelines
content:
# reusable commands & execution guidelines

## parallel task execution
run tasks, builds, or commands in parallel where appropriate to save time and optimize execution.
for example, to run tasks in the background or simultaneously in shells:
```bash
# example parallel command execution in zsh
command1 & command2 & wait
```
or run dev servers/parallel watchers as specified in workspace workflows.

title: Workflow Discovery & Search Workflow (`discovery-wf`)
content:
# workflow discovery & search workflow (`discovery-wf`)

## purpose
this workflow enables fast search and semantic matching of workflows in the workspace, reducing token usage by loading only matching files.

## execution

### 1. build or update search index
run this command to crawl `.agents/` and update the local json search index:
```bash
node scripts/discover-workflows.js --index
```

### 2. search for guidelines or workflows
run this command to search for workflows matching a specific keyword (e.g. `verify`, `plan`, `coding`):
```bash
node scripts/discover-workflows.js verify
```

## expected output
a lists of matched documents, including titles, markdown paths, and file schemes for immediate reading.

title: Documentation Refactor Workflow (`refactor-docs-wf`)
content:
# documentation refactor workflow (`refactor-docs-wf`)

## purpose
this workflow automates the reorganization of the agent guidelines and workflows into a clean subfolder hierarchy.

## execution
run the shell script to move and rename files to the correct subdirectories:

```bash
bash scripts/refactor-docs.sh
```

## expected output
all guidelines and `*-wf.md` files are moved into their respective folders (`plan/`, `impl/`, `verify/`, `debug/`, `workflows/`).

title: Repository Structure Discovery Workflow (`tree-package-wf`)
content:
# repository structure discovery workflow (`tree-package-wf`)

## execution
run the following commands to get a high-level view of the repository layout:

```bash
tree -l 3 -i 'node_modules|.git|dist|.next'
find . -maxdepth 3 -name package.json
```

## expected output
- high-level directory tree of packages and applications.
- list of package.json files identifying workspace boundaries.

## information to collect
- monorepo package structure.
- build system/workspace orchestrator configuration.

## consumption
- used during the planning phase to identify correct directories for code changes and dependencies.
